title: Brain Protocol v2 Action envelope
description: The Action primitive in full: the common record envelope (envelope.schema.json) plus the Action extension of BP-01 §5.7, the gates of BP-08 §4, and the needs_human parked state of BP-08 §3. An Action is anything that would change the world, held for consent. Lifecycle: proposed → confirmed → executed; proposed → declined | expired; proposed → needs_human → confirmed | declined; for cross-system relays confirmed → relayed → executed | failed (BP-01 §7). No transition may skip confirmed on the way to executed.

subtype.description: The action verb, named by the capability it invokes (book, send_message, entity.merge, …); validated against the grant matrix (BP-03), not the vocabulary registry (BP-01 §10.4).

summary.description: One plain sentence of what will happen — what the human reads before saying yes; generated from the payload, never free-written around it (BP-01 §5.7).

payload.description: The exact machine-readable instruction that executes on confirm. The confirm binds to a SHA-256 hash of this payload (BP-08 §2.1).

payload_hash.description: SHA-256 of the canonical payload. Every gate confirm binds to this hash; any mutation of the payload after confirm invalidates it and reverts confirmed gates to pending (BP-08 §2.1, §4.1).

requires_confirm.description: true for anything irreversible or cross-boundary (BP-01 §5.7).

proposed_by.description: Who or what drafted it, e.g. agent:garagebrain (BP-01 §5.7).

expires_at.description: Drafts go stale; an expired draft can never execute. Expiry resolves to declined:expired; re-proposing updates the existing draft, never duplicates it (BP-08 §2.1, §3.3).

state.description: The action lifecycle (BP-01 §7). executed, declined, expired and failed are terminal.

gates.description: Per-gate states for dual/multi-gated exchanges. MUST when more than one human confirm is required. Execution occurs only when ALL required gates are confirmed; any gate declining resolves the whole action to declined (BP-08 §4.1).

space.description: The space whose human this gate belongs to.

addressed_to.description: A named human, always — never a role without a person behind it, never an agent (BP-08 §3.3).

role.description: e.g. guardian — any action concerning a child gates to a guardian (BP-08 §6, CD-7).

needs_human.description: Present when state is needs_human: the parked state (BP-08 §3, CD-10). Nothing auto-resolves; silence never means yes. Default expiry: park + 7 days, resolving to declined:expired.

reason.description: Exactly one of the enumerated reasons (BP-08 §3.2). The set is closed; new reasons land by MINOR version through BP-09 governance. A receiver encountering an unknown reason treats the item as parked and surfaces it verbatim.

addressed_to.description: A named human, resolved by the parking system: the action's owner, the space's responsible adult, or a guardian where a child is concerned.

explanation.description: Plain language the addressee reads, with the context to decide.

context.description: Supporting record URNs or other context, e.g. { ours, theirs } for a disagreement.

result.description: Execution result, present once state is executed (e.g. the created appointment_ref).

allOf.$comment: When parked, the needs_human object is required (BP-08 §3.1).
